title: Warm Melted Middle Eastern Cheese Pilaf
description: A warm, cheesy, and comforting pilaf that is perfect for a midnight craving. This dish is easy to make and features a delicious combination of Middle Eastern flavors.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the smoked paprika and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the saffron-infused basmati rice and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add 2 cups of water and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes or until the rice is cooked.
While the rice is cooking, preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
Place the tandoori cheese on a baking sheet and bake for 5-7 minutes or until melted and golden brown.
Remove the cheese from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
Fluff the cooked rice with a fork and stir in the melted cheese, orange zest, and orange juice.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve hot.
